# UC-17.3.65 · Netskope First-Time Cloud Application Access by User (Shadow IT Signal)

## Description

Employees adopt new SaaS tools continuously. The first time a user accesses an unsanctioned application is the earliest moment for governance—before data volumes grow and before accounts sprawl across the tenant.

## Value

Feeds CASB governance queues with a forward-looking metric rather than waiting for DLP or malware outcomes.

## Implementation

Maintain `sanctioned_saas_apps.csv` from architecture review decisions. Use summary indexing or KV store for historical first-seen to reduce search cost on large tenants. Exclude browser update and OS background traffic via `traffic_type` filters.

## SPL

```spl
index=casb sourcetype="netskope:events" earliest=-2d
| eval app=coalesce(app_name, app, service)
| eval user=coalesce(user, user_key, os_username)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_hit dc(category) as cat_count values(category) as category by user, app
| where first_hit >= relative_time(now(), "-24h")
| lookup sanctioned_saas_apps.csv app OUTPUT sanctioned
| where sanctioned!="true" OR isnull(sanctioned)
| table user, app, category, first_hit
| sort user, app
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (user, new app, category), Bar chart (new apps per week), Funnel (discovered → reviewed → sanctioned).

## References

- [Splunkbase — Netskope Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3808)
- [Netskope — Cloud app discovery](https://www.netskope.com/products/cloud-security)
